title: ferray-core: no nditer equivalent — missing general-purpose strided iterator
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

NumPy's nditer (implemented in nditer_constr.c / nditer_api.c) is a general-purpose multi-array iterator that handles broadcasting, buffering, external loops, and operand casting in a single pass. ferray-core has no equivalent. Array iteration is done via ndarray's built-in iterators, which are single-array only. For ufunc-style operations across 2+ arrays with broadcasting, ferray-ufunc must manually broadcast_to() then zip iterators. An nditer-like facility would: (1) fuse broadcast shape computation with iteration, (2) enable external-loop optimization (hand SIMD code a contiguous chunk), (3) support multi-output operations, (4) reduce temporary allocations. This is the single biggest architectural gap between ferray-core and numpy._core.
